prompt:
[Instructions · Onboarding checklist]

> 1. Create account in IdP.
> 2. Assign `contractor` role — not `employee`.
> 3. Submit background check **before** granting VPN.
> 4. Send welcome email only after steps 1–3 complete.

Background check cleared today; IdP account exists with `employee` role. What is the next corrective step?
options: Change role to `contractor` then continue step 3–4 order

prompt:
[Instructions · Support macro]

> **Refund macro SOP**
> - Confirm order ID and delivery date.
> - If delivered >30 days ago, escalate to Tier 2 — do not refund.
> - If ≤30 days, refund shipping only on defective items; full refund on wrong item shipped.

Wrong item delivered 12 days ago. Which path applies?

[Instructions · Lab procedure]

> **Sample prep SOP**
> 1. Label tubes before opening reagent.
> 2. Incubate 10 min at 37°C — do not exceed 12 min.
> 3. Centrifuge 5 min at 3000 rpm.
> 4. Record batch ID in log before reading results.

You incubated 13 minutes. What should you do before centrifuging?
correct_answer_exact: Discard and restart; do not centrifuge this sample.
rubric: Must say discard/restart or do not proceed / repeat prep per SOP — exceeding 12 min invalidates step. Credit for not continuing to centrifuge.

prompt:
[Instructions · Shipping SOP]

> **Cold chain ship**
> - Pre-chill gel packs 24 hours.
> - Pack product only after gel packs register ≤4°C.
> - Seal with tamper tape; attach logger **before** handoff to carrier.
> - Enter tracking in TMS within 30 minutes of pickup.

Gel packs are at 6°C after 24h chill. Next step?
options: Skip logger if traffic is heavy
Hand off to carrier and enter tracking later

prompt:
[Instructions · IT change]

> **Production deploy checklist**
> 1. Announce in #changes
> 2. Snapshot DB
> 3. Deploy canary 5%
> 4. Monitor 15 min; rollback if error rate >1%
> 5. Full rollout only after canary pass

Canary error rate hits 1.2% at minute 10. Required action?

[Instructions · Finance]

> **Month-end close**
> - Lock subledgers through day T-2.
> - Post accruals only from template AC-400 series.
> - No manual journal entries without controller approval code `MC-OK`.

An accrual uses template AC-410 but lacks `MC-OK`. Post it?
options: Yes, AC-410 is in AC-400 series

prompt:
[Instructions · Event runbook]

> **Webinar go-live**
> 1. Host joins 20 min early.
> 2. Mute all attendees on entry.
> 3. Start recording only after legal disclaimer slide shown.
> 4. Q&A opens after slide 12 — not before.

Attendees ask questions during slide 8. What should the host do?
correct_answer_exact: Defer Q&A until after slide 12; keep attendees muted per SOP.
rubric: Must defer Q&A until after slide 12 per runbook; may hold questions or use chat. Wrong if opening Q&A early.

prompt:
[Instructions · Security]

> **Visitor badge SOP**
> - Photo ID required.
> - Escort mandatory on floors 2+.
> - Badge must be visible; return at exit.
> - Exception: pre-approved auditors list — escort optional on floor 2 only.

Pre-approved auditor visits floor 3 without escort. Allowed?
options: Yes, on floor 2 only per exception

prompt:
[Instructions · Customer comms]

> **Outage comms template order**
> 1. Acknowledge impact
> 2. State scope (regions/features)
> 3. Provide workaround if any
> 4. ETA or next update time
> **Do not** speculate on root cause externally.

Which sentence violates the template?
options: "We are investigating elevated errors in us-east-1."
"Likely caused by a bad deploy by engineer Alex."
"Workaround: use CSV export instead of API."

prompt:
[Instructions · Inventory]

> **Cycle count SOP**
> - Count bin before moving stock.
> - Two-person verify if variance >5%.
> - Adjust system only after supervisor sign-off in app.

Counter finds 8% variance and adjusts system immediately. What was skipped?

[Instructions · Exception path]

> **Travel booking**
> Standard: book 14+ days ahead.
> Exception: <14 days requires director email attached to expense report.
> Emergency: VP approval + incident ticket number on report.

Flight booked 5 days ahead for family event. What documentation must attach to the expense report?
correct_answer_exact: Director email attached to expense report
rubric: Director email required (<14 days, non-emergency). Must NOT say VP/incident ticket unless framed as emergency.

prompt:
[Instructions · Data deletion]

> **DSAR deletion runbook**
> 1. Verify identity ticket #.
> 2. Export audit log copy to legal bucket.
> 3. Delete primary profile rows.
> 4. Queue backup purge job — do not mark complete until job ID recorded.

Primary rows deleted; backup job running but no job ID logged. Mark DSAR complete?
options: Yes, if job will finish tonight
No, record job ID before complete

prompt:
[Instructions · Manufacturing]

> **Line changeover**
> - Stop line
> - Clear previous SKU remnants
> - QA sign-off on first article
> - Update line display SKU
> - Restart only after all four

Line display updated; first article pending QA. Restart?
options: No, wait for QA sign-off and prior steps

prompt:
[Instructions · Hiring]

> **Interview loop**
> - Recruiter screen → hiring manager → panel (2) → bar raiser optional for L5+.
> - No offer until reference check **and** background initiated.
> - Compensation only via comp band tool output attached.

Panel complete for L4 role; references pending. Can extend verbal offer?
options: No, references and background initiation required

prompt:
[Instructions · Branching SOP]

> **Incident severity**
> - If PII exposed: page security + legal immediately.
> - If revenue impact >$50k/hr: page finance director.
> - Else if customer-facing outage: page comms liaison.
> - Otherwise: standard on-call.

Customer-facing outage, no PII, $10k/hr impact. Who gets paged?
rubric: Must page comms liaison per branch (customer-facing outage) and NOT skip to finance (under $50k) or security (no PII).

prompt:
[Instructions · Procurement]

> **3-quote rule:** Purchases >$2,500 need three quotes unless single-source memo approved by procurement.
> Single-source memo must cite patent/exclusivity or emergency with VP sign-off.

$4,000 specialized OEM part; only one vendor exists (patent). Required step?

[Instructions · Content publish]

> **Blog publish checklist**
> Legal review for claims → SEO metadata → scheduled publish ET → post-publish cache purge.
> **Never** publish from draft URL directly to production DNS.

Legal cleared; metadata done. Next step before live?
options: Schedule publish ET then cache purge after live

prompt:
[Instructions · Parallel tasks]

> **Office move weekend**
> Sat: network install must finish before phone port at 18:00.
> Sun: furniture after network validation checklist signed.
> Sign-off owner: facilities only after IT checklist attached.

Phones port at 18:00 Sat; network install still in progress. Action?
options: Delay phone port until network complete and validated

prompt:
[Instructions · Escalation timer]

> **Tier 1 SOP:** Attempt KB fixes for 20 min.
> If unresolved, collect logs and escalate to Tier 2 with template T2-escalate.
> **Do not** promise refund timelines — Tier 2 only.

After 15 min KB attempts, customer demands refund ETA. Best response?
rubric: Continue KB 20 min rule OR escalate with logs if issue warrants; must not promise refund timeline as Tier 1. Credit for citing SOP boundaries.

prompt:
[Instructions · Conditional steps]

> **Equipment return**
> - If laptop: wipe via MDM then physical inspection.
> - If monitor: inspection only.
> - Charge damage fee only with photo upload in ticket.
> - Close ticket only when asset status = `returned` in CMDB.

Monitor returned; inspection OK; CMDB still `assigned`. Close ticket?
options: No, update CMDB to returned first

prompt:
[Instructions · Order of operations]

> **Batch email send**
> 1. Send test to internal list
> 2. Wait 10 min; confirm links tracked
> 3. Upload final recipient CSV
> 4. Enable send; cap 50k/hour

Final CSV uploaded; internal test skipped. Proceed?
options: No, complete test and 10-min wait first

prompt:
[Instructions · Multi-team handoff]

> **Facility leak response**
> 1. Safety: evacuate affected zone.
> 2. Facilities: shut valve.
> 3. IT: power down racks in zone after Facilities all-clear.
> 4. Comms: notify tenants after Safety all-clear.

Facilities shut valve; IT already powered racks down during evacuation. What went wrong?

[Instructions · Override rules]

> **Discount approval**
> Reps: up to 10%.
> Managers: up to 20% with CRM opportunity link.
> >20%: VP + finance tag `margin-exception`.
> **Stacking:** promo codes cannot combine with rep discount.

Rep applies 8% discount plus a 5% promo code on the same deal. Allowed? Explain.
correct_answer_exact: No — stacking promo codes with rep discount is forbidden.
rubric: Not allowed — stacking promo with rep discount is forbidden even if combined % is under manager cap.

prompt:
[Instructions · Document version]

> **SOP header:** Effective 2026-07-01 Rev C — supersedes Rev B.
> Rev B said: dispose chemical X in drain A.
> Rev C says: chemical X to hazardous waste vendor only.

Technician follows Rev B printout on July 15. Correct action?
options: Follow Rev C hazardous waste rule

prompt:
[Instructions · Time-bound exception]

> **Password reset window:** Self-service allowed 06:00–22:00 local.
> After hours: verified callback to listed mobile on file only — no email reset links.

User requests email reset link at 23:30. Proper path?
options: Verified callback to mobile on file

prompt:
[Instructions · Nested conditions]

> **Return merchandise**
> - Opened software: no return.
> - Unopened hardware ≤30 days: full refund.
> - Unopened hardware >30 days: store credit.
> - Defective: full refund any time with RMA.

Unopened laptop day 45, not defective. Outcome?

[Instructions · Audit trail]

> **Manual price override SOP**
> Enter reason code, attach approval email, update price, **then** notify billing audit channel — in that order.

Price updated and audit channel notified; reason code missing. Compliant?
correct_answer_exact: No — reason code and approval must be recorded before price update.
rubric: Not compliant — reason code and approval must precede price update per order. Must note fix: add reason/approval retroactively or redo in order.

prompt:
[Instructions · Communication order]

> **Layoff day protocol**
> 1. Employee private notification
> 2. IT access removal at notification time
> 3. Team announcement after all affected notified same day
> 4. Press statement only via corporate comms

Team announcement sent before two employees were notified. Issue?
options: Violates order — private notification must complete first

prompt:
[Instructions · Tooling gates]

> **Merge to main**
> - CI green
> - Two approvals
> - Security scan `pass` or waived ticket
> - **No merge** during change freeze without CAB ticket

CI green, two approvals, scan pass; change freeze active; no CAB ticket. Merge?

[Instructions · Compound exception]

> **Wire transfer**
> >$25k: dual approval.
> New beneficiary: call-back verification to number on file.
> International: add compliance form CF-INT.
> All three apply independently when triggered.

$30k to new international vendor. Minimum required controls?
